questions.text: Statement:I. The school authority has asked the FSC students to attend special classes to be conducted on Sundays.II. The parents of the FSC students have withdrawn their wards from attending private tuitions conducted on Sundays.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect.
options.explanation: The two statements are unrelated and independent of each other. There is no clear cause-and-effect relationship between them based on the information provided.Statement I:"The school authority has asked the FSC students to attend special classes to be conducted on Sundays."This statement indicates that the school authority has scheduled special classes for FSC students on Sundays. It does not provide any information about why these special classes are being conducted or how it relates to private tuitions.Statement II:"The parents of the FSC students have withdrawn their wards from attending private tuitions conducted on Sundays."This statement states that the parents of FSC students have decided to stop sending their children to private tuitions that were previously conducted on Sundays. However, it does not mention anything about the reason for this decision or whether it is related to the special classes organized by the school.Without additional information or context, we cannot determine any direct connection between the school's special classes and the parents' decision to withdraw their children from private tuitions on Sundays. The two statements might be independent events or have separate reasons behind them.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect

questions.text: Find the odd one.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The mother is a blood relative, sharing a direct familial bond with the person, which places her in the same category as sister and aunt.
The sister is a blood relative, being a direct family member, similar to mother and aunt.
The maid is not a blood relative but rather a non-familial domestic worker, making her the odd one out compared to the other options.
The aunt is a blood relative, connected to the family tree, like mother and sister.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Maid. In this question, mother, sister, and aunt are all directly related to an individual by blood, signifying familial connections. The maid, however, is a domestic worker with no blood or familial ties, making her distinct from the other options. Each incorrect option (Mother, Sister, Aunt) represents a family member, which excludes them from being the odd one out.

questions.text: Statement: Should children be prevented completely from watching television?Arguments:(I) No. We get vital informafion regarding education through television.(II) Yes. It hampers the study of children.(III) Yes. Young children are misguided by certain programmes featuring violence.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Only I, II and III are strong
options.explanation: This option is not correct. (II) Yes. It hampers the study of children: This argument is not strong. While excessive television watching can indeed interfere with a child's study and academic performance, it doesn't necessarily mean that children should be completely prevented from watching television. The key is to find a balance between recreational screen time and study time, rather than a complete ban.

This option is not correct. (III) Yes. Young children are misguided by certain programs featuring violence: This argument is strong. Young children are impressionable, and exposure to violent or inappropriate content on television can negatively influence their behavior and attitudes. Parents and caregivers should be cautious about the programs children watch and consider age-appropriate content to ensure their mental and emotional well-being.In conclusion, arguments I and III are strong, while argument II is not as strong. Therefore, the correct answer is: Only I and III are strong.

options.text: Only I and II are strong

The key is to find a balance between recreational screen time and study time, rather than a complete ban.

questions.text: Statement and Argument Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students? Arguments:(I) Yes. This will improve the quality of the professional institutes and degrees as they will be able to complete the degree successfully. (II) No. This will keep a large number of socially and economically backward students out of the reach of professional institutes and degrees.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Argument II is also very strong.

options.text: If only argument II is strong
options.explanation: Argument I is also strong.

options.text: If both I and II are strong.
options.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

options.text: If neither I nor II is strong
options.explanation: Both arguments are very strong.

questions.text: Statement and argument:Statement: Should the public sector undertakings be allowed to adopt a hire-and-fire policy?Arguments:(I) Yes, as this will help the public sector undertakings to get rid of non-performing employees and will also help to reward the performing employees.(II) No, the management may not be able to implement the policy in an unbiased manner and the employees will suffer due to the high-handedness of the management.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Hire and fire policy' implies 'taking up the performing employees and discarding the non-performing ones'. Clearly, such a policy would stand out to encourage employees to work hard and devotedly to retain their jobs and thus enhance productivity and profitability of the organizations. Argument II seems to be vague in the light of this.

This option is incorrect because both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument II raises concerns about potential management biases in implementing the policy.

options.text: Both arguments I and II are strong
options.explanation: Both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument I is strong because it highlights a practical benefit of adopting a hire-and-fire policy: removing non-performing employees can increase efficiency, and rewarding performing employees can improve motivation. This argument is logical and directly supports the statement. Argument II is also strong because it presents a real and reasonable concern. In many public sector undertakings, management may misuse such policies due to bias or corruption. This may lead to injustice and insecurity among employees. This argument is also logical and relevant.

options.text: Neither argument I nor II is strong
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because both arguments have reasonable points, even though they also have some flaws. It is not accurate to dismiss both arguments entirely.

questions.explanation: Both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. questions.text: How many such pairs of letters are there in the word 'HISTORICAL' which have as many letters between them in the word as they have between them in the English alphabet?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Only four pairs meet the criteria, but this is incorrect as there are six such pairs.
Correct. There are six pairs where the letters have the same number of letters between them as in the word and the English alphabet.
Only five pairs meet the criteria, which is incorrect because there are six pairs.
Incorrect. There are not as many as eight pairs; the correct number is six.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Six. The pairs that satisfy the condition are: HI, ST, IL, LS, CI, and RT. These pairs have the same number of letters between them in the word 'HISTORICAL' as they do in the English alphabet. For example, 'H' and 'I' have no letters between them in 'HISTORICAL' and none between them in the alphabet as well. Similarly, 'S' and 'T' also follow the same pattern. The other pairs can be verified similarly. Options with four, five, or eight pairs are incorrect because they do not account for all six pairs correctly.

questions.text: Choose which one is the different number from the following
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: 125 is an odd number when compared to the others, but it is a perfect cube (5^3), making it somewhat unique.
131 is a prime number, which distinguishes it from the other options that are all composite numbers.
130 is a composite number, divisible by 2 and 5, which fits in with the other composite options.
138 is also a composite number, divisible by 2 and 69, making it similar to option C.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: 131. This number is unique because it is a prime number, meaning it can only be divided by 1 and itself without resulting in a fraction. On the other hand, the other options—125, 130, and 138—are all composite numbers. 125 can be factored into 5 x 5 x 5, 130 into 2 x 5 x 13, and 138 into 2 x 3 x 23, all of which have factors other than 1 and themselves. Therefore, 131 stands out as the only prime number among the choices.

questions.text: Statements and conclusions:Statements All apples are bananas. No banana is a mango. Some mangoes are oranges.Conclusions(I) All oranges can never be bananas.(ll)Some mangoes are apples.

options.explanation: Given that no banana is a mango (Statement 2), and some mangoes are indeed shown to be oranges (Statement 3), it logically follows that those mangoes that are oranges cannot be bananas. Thus, it is correct that all oranges cannot be bananas.This conclusion is true.

This option is incorrect. Conclusion (II): Some mangoes are apples. This is incorrect. The statements indicate a relationship between apples and bananas, and bananas and mangoes, but there's no direct relationship established between mangoes and apples.

questions.text: Logical games.Select the odd word from the following:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: An herb is a type of plant that is valued for its medicinal, culinary, or aromatic properties. Herbs are a distinct category of plants and can include various species like basil, mint, or rosemary.

A flower is a reproductive structure found in many plants. It is typically colorful and is responsible for producing seeds. Flowers are a defining feature of many plant species and play a vital role in pollination.

A tree is a large woody perennial plant with a well-defined trunk, branches, and leaves. Trees are characterized by their tall height and ability to provide shade and support for various organisms. They are a distinct category of plants and can include species such as oak, pine, or maple.

A shrub is a small to medium-sized woody plant with multiple stems and a shorter height compared to trees. Shrubs often have dense foliage and can be found in various habitats. Examples of shrubs include roses, azaleas, and junipers.

questions.explanation: All options except ‘flower’ are different varieties of plants, while ‘flower’ grows on plants.Hence, ‘flower’ is the odd one out.

questions.text: Pick the word which is always associated with "books".

options.explanation: Pages: Books are made up of pages, which contain the text and information. When we think of books, we often think of flipping through the pages to read or find information.
Learning: While books are commonly used as a source of learning and knowledge, not all books are specifically intended for learning purposes. Some books may be for entertainment or other purposes.
Pictures: While many books do contain pictures, not all books have them. There are various types of books, such as novels or textbooks, where the presence of pictures may vary.
Erasers: Erasers are not typically associated with books. Erasers are used for erasing mistakes or marks made with pencils, which are more commonly associated with writing or drawing.

questions.text: A police officer stationed along the route of a parade has been ordered not to allow cars to cross the route while the parade is in progress. An ambulance driver on an emergency run attempts to drive an ambulance across the route while the parade is passing. Under these circumstances, the officer should:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Ask the driver to wait while the officer calls headquarters and obtain a decision.
options.explanation: Without any knowledge of police rules, common sense dictates that saving lives is the number one priority. An ambulance on an emergency run is on a mission to save a life. questions.text: Statement and Argument Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students? Arguments:(I) Yes. This will improve the quality of the professional institutes and degrees as they will be able to complete the degree successfully. (II) No. This will keep a large number of socially and economically backward students out of the reach of professional institutes and degrees.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Argument II is also very strong.

options.text: If only argument II is strong
options.explanation: Argument I is also strong.

options.text: If both I and II are strong.
options.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

options.text: If neither I nor II is strong
options.explanation: Both arguments are very strong.

questions.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: 'Lasan' is common to 'Lasanpoen' and 'Cuopdansa.'
Migen means cup, lasan means board.Choice B can be ruled out because migen means cup.
Poen means walk; cuop means pull, and dansa means man. The only possible choice is C.
This option is incorrect.Cuop means pull

questions.explanation: Migen means cup; lasan means board; poen means walk; cuop means pull, and dansa means man. The only possible choices, then, are choices B and C. Choice B can be ruled out because migen means cup.

questions.text: Language with the most alphabets is _
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: French uses the Latin alphabet, which consists of 26 letters.
There are As many as 50,000-60,000 characters in Chinese. Hence, Chinese is the language with the most alphabets.
This option is also incorrect because Portugese has 23 letters.
Urdu is written in the Perso-Arabic script, which includes 39 basic letters, including 3 long vowels and 36 consonants.

questions.explanation: French has 26 letters. Portuguese is 23 letter and Urdu has 36. there are As many as 50,000-60,000 characters in Chinese hence Chinese is the language with the most alphabets.

questions.text: Statement and Argument:Statement 1: The government has decided to roll back the hike in the prices of cooking gas and kerosene.Statement 2: Some ministers had resigned in protest against the hike in prices of cooking gas and other petroleum products.Select the correct option.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: I is the cause and II is effect
options.explanation: Option a) I is the cause, and II is the effect.Explanation: This option suggests that Statement 1 (the government rolling back the price hike) is the cause, and Statement 2 (some ministers resigning in protest) is its effect. In other words, the government's decision to roll back the price hike has led to some ministers resigning in protest.

options.text: II is the cause and I is the effect
options.explanation: Option B is correct , 2 is the cause and 1 is effect.

Option c) Both are independent causes.Explanation: This option suggests that both Statement 1 (the government rolling back the price hike) and Statement 2 (some ministers resigning in protest) are independent causes. It means that these two events are happening separately and are not directly related to each other as cause and effect.

options.text: Both are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Option d) Both are effects of independent causes.Explanation: This option suggests that both Statement 1 (the government rolling back the price hike) and Statement 2 (some ministers resigning in protest) are effects of independent causes. In other words, there are separate reasons or events that have caused both the government's decision to roll back the price hike and the ministers' resignations.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct, as the reduction in petroleum prices was a result of resignation of ministers.

questions.text: Verbal ClassificationWhat will the area of a triangle will be when a = lm, b = 2m, c = 3m, a, b, c being lengths of respective sides?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Put the values in the given formula and the answer will be 0 sqm.

This option is incorrect.0 sq m: The given side lengths (1m, 2m, 3m) cannot form a triangle. According to the triangle inequality theorem, the sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. In this case, 1 + 2 = 3, which is not greater than 3. Therefore, a triangle with these side lengths cannot exist, and its area is 0.

questions.text: Statement:Should coal engines be replaced by electric engines in trains?Arguments:(I) Yes. Coal engines cause a lot of pollution.(II) Yes. Electric engines are good on performance, easy to operate, and low on maintenance.(III) No. Pakistan does not produce enough electricity to fulfill its domestic needs also.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: this is not a suitable option. Argument III: "No. Pakistan does not produce enough electricity to fulfill its domestic needs also." While this might be a valid concern, it doesn't directly address the question of whether coal engines should be replaced. It introduces a practical limitation, but not a fundamental reason why electric engines are unsuitable. Therefore, it is not as strong an argument as I and II.

Arguments I and II seem to be true but argument III does not provide a convincing reason and hence does not hold strong.

questions.text: Look at this series 80, 10, 70, 15, 60. What number should come next?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is an alternating addition and subtraction series. In the first pattern, 10 is subtracted from each number to arrive at the next. In the second, 5 is added to each number to arrive at the next.First pattern (80) (80 - 10 = 70) (70 - 10 = 60) (60 - 10 = 50). Second pattern (10) (10 + 5 = 15) (15 + 5 = 20So the answer is a = 20.

This option is incorrect.This is an alternating addition and subtraction series. In the first pattern, 10 is subtracted from each number to arrive at the next. In the second, 5 is added to each number to arrive at the next.First pattern (80) (80 - 10 = 70) (70 - 10 = 60) (60 - 10 = 50). Second pattern (10) (10 + 5 = 15) (15 + 5 = 20)So the answer is a = 20.

This option is incorrect.This is an alternating addition and subtraction series. In the first pattern, 10 is subtracted from each number to arrive at the next. In the second, 5 is added to each number to arrive at the next.First pattern (80) (80 - 10 = 70) (70 - 10 = 60) (60 - 10 = 50). Second pattern (10) (10 + 5 = 15) (15 + 5 = 20So the answer is a = 20.

This option is incorrect.This is an alternating addition and subtraction series. In the first pattern, 10 is subtracted from each number to arrive at the next. In the second, 5 is added to each number to arrive at the next.First pattern (80) (80 - 10 = 70) (70 - 10 = 60) (60 - 10 = 50). Second pattern (10) (10 + 5 = 15) (15 + 5 = 20So the answer is a = 20.

questions.explanation: This is an alternating addition and subtraction series. In the first pattern, 10 is subtracted from each number to arrive at the next. In the second, 5 is added to each number to arrive at the next. First pattern (80) (80 - 10 = 70) (70 - 10 = 60) (60 - 10 = 50). Second pattern (10) (10 + 5 = 15) (15 + 5 = 20)So the answer is a = 20.

questions.text: Which of the following expressions may not be true if the following expressions are true? (a) Z > Y (b) Y = W (c) W < X
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: As we know Y=W so we can put W in place of Y in equation a, so we will get equation of this option , so its right.
We don't get this relation in any case by shuffling constants.
If we put Y in place of W in equation c , we get equation of this option, as Y=W.
As in equation a, Z is either equal to or greater than Y ,so Z=Y is true.

questions.explanation: Statement : Z ≥ Y = W and X ≥ W = Y (A) : W ≤ Z => True, since Z ≥ W. (B) : X ≥ Z => Not true (C) : Y ≤ X => True Thus, only (B) may not be true.

questions.text: Asiya goes 30 km towards North from a fixed point, then after turning to her right she goes another 15 km. Then she takes another right and goes 30km. How far and in what direction is she from her starting point?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Its better to draw coordinate axis on plane. The first 30 km north and the subsequent 15 km east create a right-angled triangle.
Option B is correct. In reference to the given image her current position is 15km East from the point where she started.
Option C (20 km east) is incorrect because after moving 30 km towards the North and taking two right turns, Asiya's final position is 15 km to the east from her starting point.
Option D (25 km east) is incorrect because her final position is 15 km east, not 25 km.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct. In reference to the given image her current position is 15km East from the point where she started.

questions.text: Statements: The increasing population of our nation will lead to the depletion of many essential resources.Conclusions:I. Population of our nation can be controlled.II. The nation will not be able to provide a decent living to its citizens.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This is incorrect. The statement does not provide any information or inference about controlling the population; it only discusses the consequences of an increasing population.

This is correct. The statement suggests that the increasing population will lead to resource depletion, implying that it will become challenging for the nation to provide a decent living to its citizens.

options.text: Either conclusion I or II follows
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Conclusion II follows directly from the statement, while conclusion I does not. Therefore, both cannot be considered equally valid conclusions.

options.text: Both conclusions I and II follow
options.explanation: This is incorrect. While conclusion II is supported by the statement, conclusion I is not addressed at all by the given information, making this option invalid.

questions.explanation: The statement directly links population growth to the depletion of essential resources. A lack of essential resources logically implies that providing a decent living for all citizens will become difficult or impossible, making Conclusion II a direct inference. The statement does not provide information about whether the population can be controlled.

questions.text: Statements: Smoking is one of the human weaknesses, which tends to test the willpower of the smoker to the edge.Conclusions:I. It is very difficult for the smokers to give up smoking even if they want to.II. Human beings have other weaknesses as well.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I follows because the statement emphasizes that smoking tests the smoker's willpower, suggesting difficulty in quitting.
Conclusion II follows because the statement refers to smoking as one of the human weaknesses, which implies the existence of other weaknesses.

options.text: Either conclusion I or II follows
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because both conclusions logically follow from the statement.

options.text: Both conclusions I and II follow
options.explanation: Both conclusions follow because the statement implies that smoking tests willpower (Conclusion I) and suggests there are other human weaknesses (Conclusion II).

questions.explanation: The phrase "tests the willpower to the edge" implies that quitting is very difficult (Conclusion I). The phrase "one of the human weaknesses" explicitly states that smoking is one among several weaknesses (Conclusion II). Both conclusions logically follow from the statement.

questions.text: Statements: Company X has a record of manufacturing cameras of quality and the latest design so that you do not spoil even a single shot irrespective of the weather conditions.Conclusions:I. No other company except X is reputed in the camera industry.II. Anyone can take an acceptable shot with camera X.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The statement does not claim that no other company is reputable, it only highlights the strengths of Company X. Therefore, conclusion I is incorrect.

The statement suggests that the quality and design of Company X's cameras allow for good shots in any weather condition, implying that anyone can take an acceptable shot with their camera. Thus, conclusion II follows.
This option implies that one of the conclusions must be true, but since conclusion I is not valid, this option is incorrect.
Since conclusion II logically follows from the statement, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The statement claims the camera's quality prevents spoiling a shot in any weather, which implies that it is easy to use for anyone (Conclusion II). The statement makes no comparison to other companies, so Conclusion I, an absolute claim about other companies, does not follow.

questions.text: Statements: Life means stresses and strains. Stresses and strains have an adverse effect on life.Conclusions:I. Life is not worth living.II. One should avoid stresses and strains in life.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Conclusion I asserts that life is not worth living, which is a subjective assertion. The premises indicate that stresses and strains adversely affect life, but they do not imply that life itself is unworthy. Hence, this conclusion is not logically supported by the premises.

Conclusion II suggests that one should avoid stresses and strains in life. This follows logically from the premises, as they state that these factors have an adverse effect on life, implying that minimizing them would be beneficial.
This option is incorrect because Conclusion II does follow from the premises. The premises suggest that avoiding stresses and strains could improve life, thereby supporting Conclusion II.

options.text: Both conclusions I and II follow
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while Conclusion II is logically supported by the premises, Conclusion I is not. The premises do not provide sufficient justification for the extreme assertion made in Conclusion I.

questions.explanation: The statements establish that stresses and strains are an inherent part of life and that they have a negative impact. It logically follows that avoiding these adverse effects would be beneficial, supporting Conclusion II. Conclusion I is an extreme, subjective judgment not supported by the statements.
